hours_formula_detail: Missouri uses the flat federal baseline of 80 hours/month (about 20 hours/week) for the general volunteer lane. The state's SNAP policy manual section 1105.035.00 (Able Bodied Adults Without Dependents (ABAWD) Work Requirements) requires ABAWDs to be 'working (80 hours or more per calendar month), and/or participating in and complying with the requirements of a qualified training program for 80 hours or more per calendar month.' The official ABAWD Volunteer Agreement form (08/2024) — Missouri's volunteer verification instrument — explicitly frames the requirement as '80 Hours of Work/Training' and provides volunteering at a 'Volunteer Agency' as one of the four ways to meet 'the 80 hours.' No SNAP-allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula appears anywhere in the Missouri general volunteer guidance. The formula-based calculation in Missouri applies, by federal default (7 CFR 273.7(m)), only to formal workfare-program participation, which is a separate lane requiring a county/state-administered agreement with the workfare site; Missouri's primary work programs are SkillUP (SNAP E&T) and Missouri Work Assistance (MWA, TANF-side). A recipient who self-initiates volunteering at any qualifying nonprofit/volunteer agency satisfies the 80-hour requirement via the ABAWD Volunteer Agreement without any state pre-approval of the site (FSD 'may contact the Volunteer Agency to verify' but does not pre-register or MOU the site).
enforced_2026_detail: Missouri enforces the ABAWD time limit statewide in FY2026. The state has no current statewide or county-level ABAWD waivers. Under HR1 / One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), Missouri implemented the federal changes effective November 1, 2025: the upper age exception increased to 65 and older (i.e., ABAWD scope expanded to adults 18 through 64); the dependent-child exception was narrowed to those caring for a child under 14 (previously under 18); and the prior exceptions for homeless individuals, veterans, and adults 24 and younger who aged out of foster care were removed. New exceptions were created for 'an Indian,' 'Urban Indian,' and 'California Indian' as defined in the Indian Health Care Improvement Act (eligible for IHS services). Existing ABAWD statuses are reevaluated at the recipient's next recertification.
minimum_wage_note: Set to 0 because the GENERAL volunteer lane in Missouri is federal_80 and does not use a wage-based formula. For reference only: the Missouri statewide minimum wage effective January 1, 2026 is $15.00/hour (up from $13.75 in 2025, per Proposition A and the Missouri Department of Labor and Industrial Relations). This figure would be used only for the separate formal workfare lane's allotment/min-wage divisor, not for general nonprofit volunteer hours.

regional_wages.label: Missouri statewide minimum wage (2026; not used in the general volunteer lane, reference only for workfare)

qualifying_org_definition: Any 'Volunteer Agency' that 'promotes job readiness and builds work experience' (per the ABAWD Volunteer Agreement form). The agency completes Section 2 of the form (job title, position description, start date, projected hours, agency name/address/phone, supervisor name/title, supervisor signature). Missouri does not maintain a statewide registry of approved volunteer sites for the ABAWD volunteer lane; there is no MOU requirement and no county pre-approval gate. FSD 'may contact the Volunteer Agency to verify the below information' — a post-hoc verification posture, not pre-clearance.
verification_instrument: ABAWD Volunteer Agreement (form revision 08/2024) — a two-section form: Section 1 (SNAP participant fills out name, DCN [departmental client number / case number], phone, DOB, address, and signs agreeing to call the agency about absences and to contact FSD if they quit) and Section 2 (the Volunteer Agency fills out job title, position description, start date, projected hours per month, agency name/phone/address, supervisor name/title, and supervisor signature). Either the participant or the volunteer agency submits to FSD per Section 3. There is no monthly attestation of hours on the form itself; hours verification is brought separately ('When a customer brings verification of hours into a resource center, staff must scan and email it to: DSS.FSD.Agreements@dss.mo.gov' — per IM-#64 (09/20/16) introducing the SkillUP rename).
submission_mechanism_detail: Per Section 3 of the ABAWD Volunteer Agreement: 'Scan and email form to: DSS.FSD.Agreements@dss.mo.gov' OR 'Mail it to: Family Support Division | 3415 Division Drive, Suite 1 | West Plains, MO 65775.' Recipients may also bring verification of hours into any local FSD Resource Center, where staff scan and email it to the same DSS.FSD.Agreements address (per IM-#64). Missouri does not have a CalFresh-style online recipient portal upload path for this specific form documented in primary sources reviewed.
submission_deadline: The ABAWD Volunteer Agreement is filed once at the start of the volunteer arrangement (binding the recipient and the agency). Verification of actual hours worked is brought in as it accumulates; primary-source guidance does not specify a fixed monthly statutory deadline, but ABAWD hours must be tracked monthly because the federal rule is satisfied on a per-calendar-month basis (80 hours/calendar month). Recipients who fall below 80 hours in a month accrue a 'non-work month' toward the 3-in-36-month time limit; after 3 non-work months, eligibility ends until the recipient re-qualifies (e.g., 30 consecutive days at 80-hour pace).
remote_stance_detail: Neither the Missouri DSS policy manual (1105.035.00) nor the ABAWD Volunteer Agreement nor the myDSS HR1 participant page reviewed addresses whether remote / online / virtual volunteer hours qualify. The form's Section 2 requires an agency address and supervisor signature but does not require the recipient's work to be performed on-site. Federal rules do not categorically exclude remote volunteering, but MO guidance is silent. Coded 'silent'.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Missouri primary source reviewed restricts the 'Volunteer Agency' to Missouri-based entities. The ABAWD Volunteer Agreement collects agency city/state/zip without limiting state. Federal default under 7 CFR 273.24 accepts any qualifying nonprofit; absent an explicit MO restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally acceptable. Coded 'silent' because no source affirmatively permits or forbids it.
required_prestep_detail: Before volunteer hours can be counted toward the 80-hour ABAWD requirement, the recipient and the volunteer agency must complete and submit the ABAWD Volunteer Agreement (form revision 08/2024) to FSD. This is NOT a state pre-approval of the site (the recipient chooses the agency; FSD does not vet or register the agency in advance) and NOT an MOU. It is a participant-initiated enrollment form binding the recipient to call about absences and to notify FSD if they quit. After the agreement is on file, FSD 'may contact the Volunteer Agency to verify the below information.' Operationally this is a low-friction one-time form per arrangement plus ongoing self-reported hour verification — not a county-administered intake or case-manager assignment.
geographic_waivers: None. Missouri does not currently hold a statewide ABAWD waiver and has no active county-level waivers in FY2026. All prior FNS-approved discretionary-exemption-type waivers expired or were terminated by USDA following the OBBBA repeal of the underlying waiver authority effective Nov 1, 2025. ABAWD time limits are enforced statewide across all 114 Missouri counties plus the City of St. Louis.
county_variation_detail: Missouri administers SNAP at the STATE level through the Department of Social Services, Family Support Division (FSD); local FSD Resource Centers operate as administrative offices of the state agency rather than independent county SNAP authorities. Policy, forms, and procedures are uniform statewide. Submissions for the ABAWD Volunteer Agreement converge on a single central address (West Plains, MO 65775) and a single email inbox (DSS.FSD.Agreements@dss.mo.gov). Coded county_variation_flag = false.
unresolved: remote_stance: no MO source confirms whether remote/online/virtual volunteer hours count toward the 80-hour ABAWD requirement.
accepts_outofstate_nonprofit: no MO source affirmatively permits or forbids an out-of-state 501(c)(3) signing the ABAWD Volunteer Agreement; coded 'silent' on federal-default reasoning only.

Whether monthly hours logs must be re-submitted on a fixed cadence vs. at recertification is not crisply specified in the primary sources reviewed; IM-#64 only documents the email scan-in pathway when recipients walk verification into a Resource Center.
Whether the new HR1/OBBBA 'Indian / Urban Indian / California Indian' exemption requires documentation of IHS eligibility is not detailed on the participant-facing myDSS page.
notes: VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22, FY2026 / OBBBA-HR1): Missouri is a clean federal_80 state. The decisive primary sources are (1) MO DSS policy manual section 1105.035.00, which defines the ABAWD work requirement as 'working (80 hours or more per calendar month), and/or participating in and complying with the requirements of a qualified training program for 80 hours or more per calendar month' — no allotment/min-wage formula appears; and (2) the official ABAWD Volunteer Agreement form (08/2024), which titles its instructions 'How to Meet 80 Hours of Work/Training' and frames volunteering as one of four pathways to '80 hours.' The volunteer agency self-attests via Section 2 (supervisor signature) and FSD verifies post-hoc; there is no state-maintained registry of approved volunteer sites, no MOU, and no county pre-approval. Because Missouri administers SNAP at the state level (not county), county_variation_flag is false. Enforcement is statewide in FY2026 with no active waivers; HR1/OBBBA changes took effect Nov 1, 2025 (age ceiling raised to 64, dependent-child exception narrowed to under-14, homeless/veteran/foster-aged-out exceptions removed, IHS-eligible Indian/Urban Indian/California Indian exception added). The Missouri equivalent of CA's CF 888 is the ABAWD Volunteer Agreement; submission paths are email (DSS.FSD.Agreements@dss.mo.gov), mail (Family Support Division, 3415 Division Drive, Suite 1, West Plains, MO 65775), or walk-in at a local FSD Resource Center. Remote-volunteer stance and out-of-state-nonprofit acceptance are both silent in MO primary sources. For reference, the MO statewide minimum wage effective Jan 1, 2026 is $15.00/hr (Proposition A) — relevant only to the separate formal workfare lane, not to the federal_80 general volunteer lane.
